title: Back-End Developer
description:
Job Title: Back-End Developer
Overview:
Join us as a Back-End Developer intern and gain hands-on experience in designing and optimizing the backbone of a dynamic platform. This internship is structured to provide educational value, mentorship, and practical learning opportunities in back-end and full-stack development. You’ll collaborate with a talented team, learn to work with AWS infrastructure, and contribute to meaningful projects that help build your technical skills and professional portfolio.
Key Responsibilities:
Under the guidance of experienced mentors, you will:
Assist in designing, building, and maintaining scalable backend systems and APIs.
Collaborate with front-end developers to understand the integration of user-facing features with server-side logic.
Learn about and support database development and optimization for high performance and reliability.
Gain experience with AWS services (e.g., EC2, Lambda, S3) for deployment and system management.
Contribute to full-stack projects, including assisting with front-end tasks using technologies like React.js.
Help develop secure and efficient APIs, gaining insight into complex data interactions.
Participate in team discussions and collaborate on transforming business and technical requirements into practical solutions.
Observe and assist in code reviews to understand high-quality and maintainable coding practices.
Qualifications:
This internship is designed to provide an educational experience. While prior experience is not mandatory, candidates with the following are encouraged to apply:
Interest in back-end development using frameworks such as Django or Node.js.
Willingness to learn full-stack development, including front-end technologies like React.js, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
Familiarity with or a desire to learn AWS cloud services (e.g., EC2, Lambda, S3).
Basic understanding of database management (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL) or NoSQL databases (e.g., Firebase, MongoDB).
Enthusiasm for learning about RESTful APIs, cloud computing, and DevOps practices.
Strong problem-solving mindset and eagerness to contribute to a team environment.
Additional Details:
Location: Remote.
Hours: Part-time, 20–30 hours per week.
Compensation: This is an unpaid internship designed to provide educational and professional development opportunities.
Learning Opportunities: Develop technical skills, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and work on real-world projects. Gain mentorship and a stronger professional portfolio to advance your career.
How to Apply:
If you’re eager to learn and grow as a developer, we’d love to hear from you! Please submit your resume and a brief statement about what you hope to gain from this internship.
